behaviors.description: The scoring model assigns the correct risk score when a transaction exceeds the configured amount threshold for its merchant category.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module boundary (category threshold config lookup + scoring rule application), not only a single pure function. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none required. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Unit): a Unit test of score math can pass while integration fails because the wrong merchant-category threshold is loaded/mapped, producing an incorrect score. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text only references configured rules, not real OS/network/subprocess; Agentic/Workflow rejected because no AI/ML judgment or multi-agent sequence is described.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real scoring-rule service to real config mapping module (in-process) with merchant-category thresholds; submit a transaction over that category limit and assert final numeric score reflects the correct category-specific threshold, not a default/other category.

behaviors.description: The risk model identifies a fraudulent transaction pattern (e.g., rapid small purchases from different geographies) even when no single rule threshold is exceeded.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP analog for AI/ML: correctness depends on real model output quality (fraud pattern detection), which stubs cannot validate. MULTI-STEP: single model decision, so not Workflow. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Integration): an Integration test with mocked/stubbed model response would always pass even if the real model fails to recognize this fraud pattern. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because behavior text specifies one prediction behavior by the risk model, not a chained multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real risk model on labeled transactions containing cross-geo rapid small-purchase fraud patterns where rule thresholds are individually under limits; assert fraud probability/label crosses detection threshold at acceptable recall for this slice.

behaviors.description: The velocity check in the scoring model correctly counts the number of transactions within the configured time window per user.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: likely boundary between transaction history provider/repo and velocity-rule logic (time-window filtering + per-user aggregation). REAL-INFRA: can be validated with in-process modules/local test store; no OS/network dependency required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Unit): a Unit test of counting logic can pass while integration fails due to repository query window semantics (inclusive/exclusive boundaries, timezone normalization) yielding wrong counts.
behaviors.test_description: Use scoring service wired to the real transaction-history query module against local test data; create edge-case timestamps around window boundaries for multiple users and assert per-user count used by velocity rule matches configured window semantics.

behaviors.description: A transaction flagged by the risk model but not by the scoring model is still held for review — the models operate as an OR gate, not AND.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is orchestration/state handoff across model outputs into a decision gate; single-function unit tests can miss wiring mistakes (wrong boolean composition). REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP: assertion is gate logic, not model-quality judgment; model outputs can be deterministic fixtures here. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agent chain needed. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Unit): Unit test of OR helper passes, but integration can fail if one model flag field is miswired/misnamed so hold decision behaves like AND.
behaviors.test_description: Wire scoring output, risk output, and review-decision module together; feed case (scoring=false, risk=true) and assert transaction status becomes held-for-review.

behaviors.description: The combined output includes both the numeric risk score and the risk model's fraud probability, so the reviewer can see which model flagged the transaction and why.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is typically at module contract/serialization boundary when combining outputs from two model components into reviewer payload. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is necessary to catch this contract issue. LLM-DEP: this checks presence/correct mapping of fields, not model-quality correctness. MULTI-STEP: single aggregation step, not workflow. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (above Unit): Unit test of one serializer function may pass, while integration fails because upstream field names/types differ and one signal is dropped in assembled payload.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end in-process assembly path from both model result objects into review DTO/API payload; assert payload contains numeric score, fraud probability, and per-model flag provenance fields with correct values/types.
